Women, Infants, and Children (WIC)
Women, Infants, and Children (WIC)
Welcome to the Napa County WIC Program!
WIC (Women, Infants and Children) is a national nutrition education program that promotes healthy eating, encourages and supports breastfeeding, and provides referrals to health and community resources. WIC Participants receive monthly food benefits to buy healthy food, tips for healthy eating and active living, support and help with breastfeeding, and referrals to other community programs. WIC is here to help your family with convenient hours and locations in the cities of Napa, American Canyon, Calistoga, and St. Helena.
Eligibility
To see if you qualify to receive WIC benefits, call WIC to be screened over the phone or come into the clinic to meet with a staff member who can determine your eligibility. The requirements for the eligibility can be found at
How Can I Get WIC?
website.
Cost
WIC services are available free of charge for those that meet the eligibility requirements below. Staff members can assist you with the application process. Please
contact WIC
to schedule an appointment.
Participation
You can participate in the WIC Program if you:
Are in a category served by WIC
Pregnant
Breastfeed a baby under one year of age
Just had a baby in the past six months
Have children under five years of age. Including those cared for by a single father, grandparents, foster parent, step parent or guardian
Meet the
WIC income guidelines
for your family
Provide document that you live in California
Take the online
WIC Eligibility Assessment
to see if you might qualify. The online screening tool is just a preliminary assessment and does not guarantee that you will qualify for the WIC Program.
